english: resp. sku - mdog (sku mdog), colour (cf. k'a-dóg (kha dog)) B., C.; mdog-légś (mdog legs) of a beautiful colour; mdog-mdzéś (mdog mdzes) 1. id., 2. a rose. Cs.; mdog-dkar-k'á (mdog dkar kha) perh. the more corr. spelling for *do-gar-k'á* (*do gar kha*) W., light-blue; mdog-gsál (mdog gsal) a species of gall, lit. 'purifier of the skin', Med.

1. the white spot, blaze, star on the forehead of a horse Glr. and elsewh.; 2. the eye in a peacock's feather; rmá-byai mdoṅs (rma byai mdongs), sgro-mdoṅs (sgro mdongs), mdoṅs-sgro (mdongs sgro) peacock's feather; mdoṅs-mt'a-ċan (mdongs mtha' can) turkey-hen Cs.
= ldoṅ-ba (ldong ba), blind, physically and morally, B., mig-mdoṅs-pa (mig mdongs pa), mdoṅs-par ˳gyúr-ba (mdongs par 'gyur ba), to get blind, to be made blind Dzl.

Mil., mdoṅs-sól żú-ba (mdongs sol zhu ba) or byéd-pa (byed pa) Cs., to congratulate, to wish joy to another Cs.; Zam. explains it by ˳dún-pa ('dun pa) to wish, another Lex. by ṅó-dga (ngo dga) joy; in the passage of Mil. it seems to signify thank-offering.
a cross formed of two small sticks, the ends of which are connected by coloured strings, and used in various magic ceremonies.

1. Sch.: 'clay; cleaving, adhesive, sticky.' In C. = ˳jím-pa ('jim pa) (W. *ká-lag* (*ka lag*)) a mixture of clay and water; ˳dag-żál ('dag zhal) S.g. prob. id.; ˳dág-pa sbyáṅ-ba ('dag pa sbyang ba) to make such a mixture, Cs.; ˳dag-sbyár ('dag sbyar) covering, or stopping up with clay, e.g. the chinks of a wall or door, *˳dag - jár ˳búl - ba* (*'dag jar 'bul ba*) to render such service to a meditating Lama as an act of piety. In Pth. ˳dág-pa ('dag pa) is mentioned as a kind of plastic art, and evidently signifies to mould, to model, to shape. — 2. = ldág-pa (ldag pa) Cs.: ˳dág-gu ('dag gu) Lex. = skyó-ma (skyo ma), pap, pulp, prob. = ldé-gu (lde gu). — 3. pf. dag (dag), 1. to clear, to wash away, to wipe off, dri-ma (dri ma), frq.; rtá-la sol-byúg (rta la sol byug) (to clean) a horse marked or blackened with charcoal Glr.; sdig-sgrib (sdig sgrib) (to wash off) the filth of sin Glr. 2. to disappear, of sinful thoughts Glr., sometimes gnáś-su (gnas su) to their own place, is added pleon. Mil. — Participle dág-pa (dag pa) clean, v. dág-pa (dag pa).
Sch. to come to, to arrive at; cf. also brgya-˳daṅs (brgya 'dangs), sub brgya (brgya).
a train of persons, ˳k'or-˳dab ('khor 'dab) retinue Cs.

1. wing, sprúg-pa (sprug pa) to shake (the wings) Cs., rgyób-pa (rgyob pa) to clap them Cs. — 2. ladle, float-board of a water-wheel. — 3. petal, flower-leaf, frq.; ˳dab-brgyad ('dab brgyad) eight-petaled Glr.; v. Schl. Buddh. 248. — 4. any leaf, a broad leaf, also lo-˳dab (lo 'dab). — 5. fan Cs. — 6. flag Cs. — ˳dab-čags ('dab chags) a winged animal, bird, frq. — ˳dab-ráṅs-pa ('dab rangs pa) full of leaves; with leaves fully developed Sch. — ˳dab-gšóg ('dab gshog) flag-feather, quill-feather.

rarely ˳dab ('dab), the side, lateral surface, of a hill, of the body etc.; surface, mčín-˳dabs (mchin 'dabs) of the liver Med.; in a more general sense: sgál-˳dabs (sgal 'dabs) the lumbar region Med.; pleon.: nágs-˳dabs-na (nags 'dabs na) = nágs-na (nags na) in the woods Mil.

mud, mire, swamp, earth and water, = ˳dág-pa ('dag pa), but as a product of nature; ˳dam-rdzáb ('dam rdzab) B., *˳dam-tsóg* (*'dam tshog*) W. id.; ˳dám-du ('dam du), ˳dam-rdzáb-la ('dam rdzab la) byíṅ-ba (bying ba) to sink into a swamp; *˳dam - pág(s)* (*'dam pag(s)*) W. muddy plash, slough. — ˳dám-bu ('dam bu) reed for thatching, writing etc.; Cs. also sugar-cane; ˳dam-bu ka-ra? ('dam bu ka ra?) prob. a species of reed in wells or ponds Wdn.; *˳dam-búr* (*'dam bur*) W. sugar-cane.

Zam., ˳dám-ga ('dam ga), ˳dám-ṅa ('dam nga), ˳dám - pa ('dam pa) Cs. choice, option, deṅ saṅ ˳dám-ka byéd - pa (deng sang 'dam ka byed pa) to choose whether to-day or to-morrow Zam.; cf. gdám-ka (gdam ka).

(or ˳dóm(s)-pa ('dom(s) pa) Glr. prov.) pf. ˳dams ('dams), imp. ˳dom(s) ('dom(s)), to choose, to select, a bride Glr.; mi-gtsáṅ-ba ˳dám-pa (mi gtsang ba 'dam pa) such as choose impure things, cynical, lascivious characters Stg.; ˳dam-ríṅ ('dam ring) choosing, turning over in one's mind a long while; dgrá-bo gáṅ-pa mi gtaṅ ˳dam - ríṅ tábs - kyis gdul (dgra bo gang pa mi gtang 'dam ring tabs kyis gdul) prob.: not losing sight of your enemy, constantly watching, put him
